project.objective: Identify the parents of Patrick Flynn, born ca. 1845 in Pennsylvania, died 1908 in Schuylkill County, PA

questions.question: Was Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry, County Tipperary, the father of Patrick Flynn (b. ~1845, d. 1908)?
questions.rationale: An unsourced FamilySearch Family Tree names Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry, Co. Tipperary (b. ~1800) as Patrick's father. The claim has been carried by several online trees but cites no record. This question tests it against primary evidence before any of it is accepted into our tree.

items.rationale: Locate Patrick as a child and establish his birth year.
Check the Ballingarry parish registers for Thomas Flynn — the specific man the online tree names as Patrick's father.
Patrick's death certificate corroborates his birth year.

log.notes: Patrick Flynn, age 5, in the household of Bridget Flynn (widow), Schuylkill Co. Birth ~1845, Ireland. No adult male Flynn in the household.

Ballingarry (Co. Tipperary) Catholic parish burial register: Thomas Flynn, of Ballingarry, buried 14 November 1842. Identifiers (townland, age ~42, wife Bridget) match the Thomas Flynn the online tree names as Patrick's father. This is the man the claim refers to — and he died nearly three years before Patrick was born.
Death certificate: Patrick Flynn, d. 12 March 1908, Schuylkill Co.; age at death given as ~63, implying birth ~1845. Corroborates the 1850 census birth year.

sources.citation: 1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, dwelling 142, family 150, Bridget Flynn household; NARA microfilm publication M432, roll 810; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 2 May 2026.
sources.notes: Patrick age 5 in the household of widow Bridget Flynn. No adult male Flynn present.

sources.citation: Ballingarry (County Tipperary, Ireland), Catholic parish registers, burials, Thomas Flynn, 14 November 1842; National Library of Ireland, Catholic Parish Registers; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 4 May 2026.
sources.notes: Burial of Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry, 1842 — the man the online tree names as Patrick's father.

sources.citation: Pennsylvania Department of Health, death certificate no. 4521 (1908), Patrick Flynn; Pennsylvania State Archives, Harrisburg; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 6 May 2026.

citation_detail.who: Pennsylvania Department of Health; informant: James Brown (son-in-law)

sources.notes: Corroborates Patrick's birth year (~1845). Father not named.

assertions.informant: Unknown informant (most likely a household member, possibly a neighbor) reporting to census enumerator
assertions.informant_bias_notes: The subject as a child, in his widowed mother's household.

assertions.informant: Unknown — most likely a household member, possibly a neighbor
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Age 5 in 1850 implies birth ~1845. Even allowing for census age error, Patrick was born no earlier than ~1843.
The decisive refuting fact: the Thomas Flynn the online tree names as Patrick's father was buried in 1842, ~3 years before Patrick was born.
Identifies the burial as that of Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry — matching the townland, approximate age, and wife (Bridget) named in the online tree, establishing this is the man the claim refers to.
Birth year derived from age at death; corroborates the 1850 census in placing Patrick's birth ~1845.

person_evidence.rationale: Patrick Flynn, age 5, Schuylkill Co., birth ~1845 — the subject as a child. Corroborated by the 1908 death certificate.
Burial of Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry, 1842 — identifiers (townland, age, wife Bridget) match the Thomas Flynn the online tree names as Patrick's father.
Same burial record establishes the identity of Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry (I2), the candidate father named by the online tree.
Death certificate, d. 1908 Schuylkill Co. — unambiguously the subject.

1850 census age (5) for Patrick Flynn, Schuylkill Co. — the same enumeration whose name assertion (a_001) already identifies I1. Grounds his ~1845 birth, the fact the disproof turns on; the 1908 death-certificate age (a_006) corroborates the same birth year.

hypotheses.claim: Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry, County Tipperary, was the father of Patrick Flynn
hypotheses.ruled_out_reason: Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry was buried on 14 November 1842 (Ballingarry Catholic parish burial register). Patrick was born ~1845 (1850 census age 5; corroborated by the 1908 death certificate). A man who died in 1842 cannot have fathered a child born ~1845 — the death predates the birth by nearly three years. The parentage claim, which originates from an unsourced online family tree, is chronologically impossible.
hypotheses.notes: This claim was carried by several online FamilySearch trees with no supporting record. It is refuted, not merely unsupported.

events.description: Born in Ireland, ~1845 (1850 census age; corroborated by death certificate)
Age 5 in the household of widow Bridget Flynn
